rulings.Case Title: MURPHY PROPERTIES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. USA WASTE OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Murphy Properties Inc., A California Corporation'S Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement And For Recovery Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MURPHY PROPERTIES INC.'S Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement And For Recovery Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses. The motion is denied. The Court finds that the parties settled the alleged breaches that occurred in December 2011, plaintiff did not reserve the fee issue for later adjudication, there is no prevailing party with regard to the December 2011 alleged breaches, and plaintiff failed to move for fees within a reasonable time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Of Gordon & Rees And Andrew Castricone For Terminating, And In The Alternative, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Pltfs' Violation Of Court Orders
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs GORDON & REES LLP and ANDREW CASTRICONE's Motion Of Gordon & Rees And Andrew Castricone For Terminating, And In The Alternative, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Pltfs' Violation Of Court Orders Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The motion is unopposed. The court is considering a terminating sanction. A hearing is required to review all relevant matters which support that finding. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of John Thomason And Award Of Monetary Sanctionsa Gainst Deft And Its Counsel Littler Medelson In The Amount Of $1915.0
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 21, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of John Thomason And Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Counsel Littler Mendelson In The Amount Of $1915. Denied. Plaintiff has failed to overcome the presumption of confidentiality between executive John Thomason and in-house counsel. Defendant has demonstrated that the dominant purpose of the communications between in-house counsel and Mr. Thomason and other Bank of the West executives related to Plaintiff was as lawyer and client. (Clark v. Sup.Ct. (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 37, 51.) All relevant communications were made in furtherance of the attorney-client and are thus, protected from disclosure. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHARMAN SPECTOR VS. AUDREY SPECTOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract. Cross-complainants have leave to attach a copy of the contract/s, plead their terms in haec verba, or plead the terms of the contract/s according to their legal effect. In addition, if cross-complainants seek to allege the breach of multiple contracts, each contract supports a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the second cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend to plead the claim with more specificity. Cross-defendant did not demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for conversion is overruled. In PCO, Inc. v. Christensen, Miller, Fink, Jacob, et al. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 384, 396, the court explains: California cases permitting an action for conversion of money typically involve those who have misappropriated, commingled, or misapplied specific funds held for the benefit of others. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend to clarify exactly what portions of the parties' agreement/s the cross-complainants seek a judicial determination regarding. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for injunctive relief is sustained with leave to amend to allege the claim for an injunction as a remedy/prayer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHARMAN SPECTOR VS. AUDREY SPECTOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT SHARMAN SPECTOR'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the claim for attorney's fees. The cross-complainants fail to cite a statutory or contractual basis for the claim. The remainder of the motion is denied. The cross-complaint as a whole has sufficient allegations regarding the special damages. The cross-complaint pleads sufficient facts regarding cross-defendant's breaches of fiduciary duty that support the claim for punitive damages. At 11:15-20, the cross-complaint alleges physical injury and while the recovery of emotional distress damages is dubious, it is premature to strike the claim. The Court denies cross-complainant's request for judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GRANVILLE MCCOLLOUGH VS. SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ROSS MIRKARIMI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained. The demurrer to causes of action three, five, and six is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The complaint does not adequately plead compliance with the Government Claims Act. (Gov. Kliger (1990) 52 Cal.3d 65, 77.) rulings.Case Title: MEHRAN GOLCHEHREH et al VS. SAEED GOLCHEHREH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Fred K Sepapour From Representing Mehran Golchehreh And Safieh Golchehreh Adverse To Deft'S In This Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INC.'S MOTION To Disqualify Fred K Sepapour From Representing Mehran Golchehreh And Safieh Golchehreh Adverse To Defendants In This Action. Granted. Counsel Fred K. Sepapour is disqualified from representing Plaintiffs in this action. Absent informed written consent, a lawyer may not represent a client in litigation against a former client that bears a "substantial relationship" to the earlier proceedings. City & County of San Francisco v. Cobra Solutions, Inc. (2006) 38 Cal.4th 839, 847. A "substantial relationship" exists whenever the subject matter of the prior and the current representations are linked in some rational manner. Flatt v. Sup. Ct. (Daniel) (1994) 9 Cal.4the 275, 283. The moving party must disclose detailed facts showing the acquisition of confidential information. Faughn v. Perez (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 592, 601. The court finds that a conflict exists. Counsel formerly represented Defendant Food & Liquor as to its incorporation and counsel likely obtained confidential information. Moreover, it is clear that counsel had an ongoing relationship with several of the family members who are engaged in this matter and are adverse to one another. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MEHRAN GOLCHEHREH et al VS. SAEED GOLCHEHREH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 30 days leave to amend, as the entire complaint for uncertainty and for failure to plead sufficient facts as to Count One and Count Two. (CCP § 430.10(e), (f) & (g).) Overruled as to the demurrer of the entire complaint for lack of capacity and misjoinder and for failure to plead facts as to Count Three. (CCP §§ 430.10(a) & (d).) Plaintiff Safieh's statements do not amount to facts sufficient for the Court to determine that Plaintiff Safieh lacks the legal capacity to sue. (See Prob. Code §§810, 811.) The Complaint lacks designations for each count pursuant to CRC 2.112, and Plaintiffs therefore fail to inform Defendant which claims are being asserted against it. Misjoinder of parties is not present on the face of this Complaint. (CCP § 379.) For Count One, conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 514.) Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations as general allegations. For Count Two, Plaintiffs have not alleged whether the contract is written, oral, or implied by conduct. (CCP § 430.10(g).) For Count Three, an insufficient verification is not a proper grounds for demurrer. (Butterfield v. Graves (1902) 138 Cal. 155, 158.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: MEHRAN GOLCHEHREH et al VS. SAEED GOLCHEHREH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANTS SAEED GOLCHEHREH, DAVID GOLCHEHREH, SUZANNE GOLCHEHREH AND SEPEDAH GOLCHEHREH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 30 days leave to amend, the demurrer as to Count One, Count Two, and Count Three. (CCP § 430.10(b), (e) & (g).) For Count One, conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 514.) P laintiffs are granted leave to amend to allege the conspiracy allegations as general allegations. For Count Two, Plaintiffs have not alleged whether the contract is written, oral, or implied by conduct. (CCP § 430.10(g).) For Count Three, although Plaintiff has sufficiently pled a cause of action for involuntary dissolution (CCP § 430.10(e)), Plaintiff Safieh has not clearly alleged in what capacity she is bringing this cause of action. (CCP § 430.10(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG HOLLIS VS. UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 2, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC., CHRISTIAN AGARDI, JEFF ELLIS AND MIKE LANCASTER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the Complaint's second cause of action for Hostile Work Environment Harassment in Violation of FEHA as to all Defendants and sixth cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress as to individual Defendants Agardi, Ellis, and Lancaster is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to individual Defendants Ellis and Lancaster because the Complaint fails to allege that the conduct of these individual defendants was "severe or pervasive" or that their conduct fell outside the "realm of properly delegated personnel management authority." Janken v. GM Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 55, 79. The demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled as to individual Defendant Agardi and Defendant Universal Service Group, Inc. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action is overruled as to individual Defendant Agardi. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
